name: Tobacco Smoking
definition: Tobacco smoking is an exposure involving inhalation of combustion products that can affect respiratory, cardiovascular, immune, and cancer-related biological pathways. GENARCH describes smoking as a population-level environmental driver in gene–environment research; it does not provide medical advice or individual risk prediction.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Smoke exposure contributes to airway irritation, oxidative stress, and chronic inflammatory remodeling in population studies.
Smoking is associated with endothelial dysfunction and atherosclerotic processes through inflammatory and oxidative pathways.
Immune modulation and altered inflammatory signaling can occur; effects are context-dependent across tissues and time.

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: Initiation and cumulative exposure can begin in adolescence, influencing population-level trajectories of respiratory and cardiovascular outcomes.

references.title: WHO report on the global tobacco epidemic
The Health Consequences of Smoking—50 Years of Progress
references.journal: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
